description_long: This is an ornate, vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared brass horn featuring decorative embossed patterns. The base is made of dark wood with intricate metallic embellishments and a wavy, contoured edge, giving it a luxurious, antique appearance. The turntable platter is centrally located and appears to be made of black material, with a visible record on top. The tonearm is metallic and rests on the side of the platter, with a small stylus at its end. The front of the base displays a metallic plaque with the word 'LONDON' engraved, adding to its historical charm. The overall finish is a mix of polished brass and dark wood, with detailed craftsmanship evident in the decorative motifs.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wood base, and decorative metallic accents. The turntable and tonearm are prominently featured, with a 'LONDON' plaque on the front.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the flared brass horn and the ornate wood base with metallic decorations, showing the tonearm resting on the platter.
description_view_1: The rear view centers the horn and shows the back of the tonearm and the record on the platter, with less detail on the base's front decorations.
description_view_2: The front view displays the 'LONDON' plaque, the full turntable platter, and the decorative front face of the base, with the horn extending to the right.
description_view_3: The top-down view looks directly into the horn, showing the circular opening and the platter with the record, as well as the symmetrical design of the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and centrally located on the base. It appears to be made of black material, possibly plastic or metal, and is slightly raised above the base. A record is visible on top, and the platter has a smooth, matte finish with no visible grooves or patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and extends from the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is medium in length, slightly curved, and appears to be resting on the record. The finish is metallic, matching the brass and gold tones of the horn.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible at the end of the tonearm, small and pointed, likely made of metal. It is attached as a separate component to the tonearm and positioned over the record.
